rooms.name: Spur Tower -- Main Hall
rooms.description: A broad stone hall with Domnathar-precise geometry -- every angle exact, every join invisible. Orekan staff move between desks and filing cabinets with quiet efficiency. The air smells of old paper and lamp oil. A directory board lists departments in three scripts: Domnathar formal, Eke Concordant standard, and river-trader shorthand. Stairs lead up into administrative levels that the directory does not list.

textures: stone floors worn smooth by centuries of foot traffic
ambient_details: A clerk carries a stack of files taller than her head.
Someone coughs in a back office. The sound echoes longer than it should.

rooms.name: Spur Tower -- Main Archives
rooms.description: Floor-to-ceiling shelves line every wall, packed with bound ledgers, scrolled correspondence, and filing boxes labeled in a hand that hasn't changed in decades. A Halfling sits at the central desk, surrounded by a fortress of organized paperwork. A heavy door at the back bears a small depression in its frame -- a resonance lock, currently sealed. A note sits on the desk in neat, unsigned handwriting.

sounds: a faint hum from behind the sealed door
textures: the shelves vibrate faintly -- resonance trapped in forty years of sealed documents
ambient_details: A note on the desk catches the lamplight. The handwriting is precise, unhurried, and unsigned.
The sealed door hums at a frequency you can feel in your Kin-sense but cannot name.

npc_relevance.9511: Heln runs this archive. She takes instructions from the person upstairs she has never met.

rooms.name: Spur Tower -- Restricted Stacks
rooms.description: The resonance lock unsealed with a click you felt through your bones. Inside: decades of correspondence, research notes, and intelligence summaries filed with inhuman precision. Every document is cross-referenced. Every date is exact. The filing system is itself a kind of mind -- patient, thorough, and forty years deep. Your Kin-sense hums in here. The sealed documents still carry traces of the hands that filed them.

sounds: silence so deep it presses on the ears
textures: every drawer pulls smoothly, as if oiled yesterday
ambient_details: A filing drawer labeled 'Farborn Inquiry -- Year 1697-1700' sits at eye level.
The handwriting on every label is the same. Forty years of the same hand.

rooms.name: Spur Tower -- Envoy-Scholar Quarter
rooms.description: A residential corridor off the main hall where visiting scholars and Domnathar envoys keep quarters. The rooms are modest but well-maintained -- stone walls softened with tapestries that depict river trade and mountain passes. A Domnathar man and a Farborn woman share a doorway at the end of the hall. He is talking. She is listening with the careful stillness of someone who has learned to be quiet in a place where she does not quite belong.

sounds: muffled conversation behind a closed door
the scratch of a pen on paper
smells: cooking spices unfamiliar to this region -- Long Road spices
textures: the stone floor is warm here, heated from below
ambient_details: A stack of unsent letters sits on a windowsill, weighted with a river stone.
The tapestries show scenes of the Long Road. Someone hung them recently.

npc_relevance.9501: Sarre Indorach lives here with Maeren. He is friendly, eager to discuss research.
npc_relevance.9502: Maeren is here. Guarded. Quiet. The Long Road spices are hers.

rooms.name: Spur Tower -- Upper Stairs
rooms.description: A narrow stone staircase spiraling upward into the administrative levels the directory downstairs does not list. The air changes as you climb -- colder, drier, the smell of old stone replacing lamp oil. Kin-sense flickers here. Something above registers as void -- deliberate, predatory wrongness -- and then it's gone, as if whatever is up there noticed you noticing and withdrew.

sounds: your footsteps echo upward and do not come back
textures: the banister is dust-free but cold, as if cleaned by hands that don't leave warmth
ambient_details: A flicker of void on your Kin-sense, then nothing. Like a candle blown out in a room you can't see into.

rooms.name: Spur Tower -- Records Adjutant's Office
rooms.description: The door is open. That's wrong. It has been closed for decades. Inside: a desk, a chair, filing cabinets that match the ones downstairs but older. The lamp is lit. The chair is occupied. A figure sits behind the desk with the patience of someone who has been waiting -- not for you specifically, but for the inevitability of someone like you. Your Kin-sense floods with void. Deliberate. Predatory. Wrong. He looks up. He is not surprised.

sounds: the scratch of a pen stopping
textures: the air is thick with void resonance, like standing in a bruise
ambient_details: The desk is perfectly organized. Every document aligned. Every drawer labeled. Forty years of a man's life, filed with inhuman precision.

npc_relevance.9500: Vaerix Caelnorath. 280 years old. He has been waiting.
